perturbation.original_text: 5.1 Orders. Distributor shall order Products from Hydraspin by submitting purchase orders and an installation plan identifying the number(s) and type(s) of Products ordered, the requested delivery date(s) (each, an "Order"). The requested delivery date shall be no less than 120 days from the date the Order is accepted by Hydraspin. Hydraspin will not unreasonably reject any Order for Products. Hydraspin will accept or reject each Order submitted by Distributor within ten (10) days after receipt of the Order. Any Orders not expressly rejected by Hydraspin within such ten (10) day period shall be deemed accepted by Hydraspin. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Distributor may cancel any Order for Products that are not delivered within sixty (60) days after the delivery date specified therein.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 Orders. Distributor shall order Products from Hydraspin by submitting purchase orders and an installation plan identifying the number(s) and type(s) of Products ordered, the requested delivery date(s) (each, an "Order"). The requested delivery date shall be no less than 120 days from the date the Order is accepted by Hydraspin. Hydraspin will not unreasonably reject any Order for Products. Hydraspin will accept or reject each Order submitted by Distributor within ten (10) days after receipt of the Order. Any Orders not expressly rejected by Hydraspin within such ten (10) day period shall be deemed accepted by Hydraspin. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Distributor may cancel any Order for Products that are not delivered within thirty (30) days after the delivery date specified therein.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the cancellation clause from 60 days to 30 days after the delivery date, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the cancellation timeframe. This directly contradicts the provision stating that the delivery date must be at least 120 days after the order is accepted. If the distributor can cancel after only 30 days of non-delivery, it undermines the initial 120-day delivery timeframe, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes. The contradiction lies in the fact that Hydraspin is given a 120 day period to deliver, and the distributor now has only 30 days to cancel after the expected delivery date. This undermines the initial agreement for delivery time, and sets the two parties in a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Orders. Distributor shall order Products from Hydraspin by submitting purchase orders and an installation plan identifying the number(s) and type(s) of Products ordered, the requested delivery date(s) (each, an "Order"). The requested delivery date shall be no less than 120 days from the date the Order is accepted by Hydraspin. Hydraspin will not unreasonably reject any Order for Products. Hydraspin will accept or reject each Order submitted by Distributor within ten (10) days after receipt of the Order. Any Orders not expressly rejected by Hydraspin within such ten (10) day period shall be deemed accepted by Hydraspin. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Distributor may cancel any Order for Products that are not delivered within thirty (30) days after the delivery date specified therein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the cancellation clause from 60 days to 30 days after the delivery date, while the contradicted text states that the requested delivery date shall be no less than 120 days from the date the order is accepted. This directly creates a conflict, as the distributor can now cancel after only 30 days of non-delivery after the delivery date, effectively reducing the amount of time Hydraspin has to deliver the product when the contract specifies that the date shall be 'no less than 120 days'. This introduces an inconsistency as it shortens the effective timeframe for delivery and undermines the 120-day delivery timeframe and can be grounds for legal disputes.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 No Cost. Hydraspin shall provide the Products to Distributor's Customers at no cost to Distributor or the Customers, except solely for the Hydraspin Share of Net Revenue, which shall be due and payable to Hydraspin as set forth in this Article VI. Likewise, Hydraspin shall install, service and maintain the Products, with the commercially reasonable assistance of Distributor, at no cost to Distributor or the customers except solely for the Hydraspin Share of Net Revenue, which shall be due and payable to Hydraspin as set forth in this Article VI.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 No Cost. Hydraspin shall provide the Products to Distributor's Customers at no cost to Distributor or the Customers, except solely for the Hydraspin Share of Net Revenue, which shall be due and payable to Hydraspin as set forth in this Article VI. Likewise, Hydraspin shall install, service and maintain the Products, with the commercially reasonable assistance of Distributor, at a cost not to exceed 10% of the product cost to Distributor or the customers except solely for the Hydraspin Share of Net Revenue, which shall be due and payable to Hydraspin as set forth in this Article VI.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text changes the original clause that stated that Hydraspin would install, service, and maintain the products "at no cost" by specifying that these activities will be provided at a cost not exceeding 10% of the product cost. This creates an in-text contradiction because it introduces a cost element where previously there was none, and creates uncertainty as to what the "Hydraspin Share of Net Revenue" is and is no longer clear on whether that includes the installation, service and maintenance of the products. This creates uncertainty and disputes over financial obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 No Cost. Hydraspin shall provide the Products to Distributor's Customers at no cost to Distributor or the Customers, except solely for the Hydraspin Share of Net Revenue, which shall be due and payable to Hydraspin as set forth in this Article VI. Likewise, Hydraspin shall install, service and maintain the Products, with the commercially reasonable assistance of Distributor, at a cost not to exceed 10% of the product cost to Distributor or the customers except solely for the Hydraspin Share of Net Revenue, which shall be due and payable to Hydraspin as set forth in this Article VI.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Hydraspin shall install, service, and maintain the products at 'no cost'. The changed text modifies this, stating that these activities will be provided at a cost 'not to exceed 10% of the product cost'. This directly contradicts the original 'no cost' provision, creating a clear and significant financial inconsistency within the contract. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as it introduces conflicting obligations regarding cost. The contradicted text is in the same location, as the modified text changes the meaning of the original text.

perturbation.original_text: 10.3 Effect of Termination. (a) Termination or expiration of this Agreement will not relieve either Party of any obligation incurred hereunder prior to such termination or expiration. Each Party will be entitled to cancel any outstanding Orders, to the extent Products have not been delivered. Hydraspin shall be entitled to retrieve its Products from the Customers at its sole cost and expense. (b) If termination is the result of a Change of Control, Distributor shall be entitled to receive a onetime payment, within three (3) business days of the effective date of the Change of Control, equal to the greater of the following 1) the aggregate amount of the Distributor Share received during the 18 months prior to the effective date of such Change of Control or 2) the aggregate amount of the Distributor Share received on the 30 days prior to the effective date of such Change of Control multiplied by 18.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.3 Effect of Termination. (a) Termination or expiration of this Agreement will not relieve either Party of any obligation incurred hereunder prior to such termination or expiration, except that Distributor may be relieved of obligations to make future payments, if applicable. Each Party will be entitled to cancel any outstanding Orders, to the extent Products have not been delivered. Hydraspin shall be entitled to retrieve its Products from the Customers at its sole cost and expense. (b) If termination is the result of a Change of Control, Distributor shall be entitled to receive a onetime payment, within thirty (30) business days of the effective date of the Change of Control, equal to the greater of the following 1) the aggregate amount of the Distributor Share received during the 18 months prior to the effective date of such Change of Control or 2) the aggregate amount of the Distributor Share received on the 30 days prior to the effective date of such Change of Control multiplied by 18.
perturbation.explanation: The modification in 10.3(a) introduces an exception allowing the Distributor to be relieved of future payment obligations upon termination, which creates a conflict with the initial statement that termination will not relieve either party of any obligation incurred prior to termination. Further, the modification changes the timeframe for the one-time payment to the Distributor in 10.3(b) from "within three (3) business days" to "within thirty (30) business days", contradicting the original expedited payment schedule. This in-text contradiction makes the payment terms vague and confusing, and leads to disputes over financial obligations upon the Change of Control.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.3 Effect of Termination. (a) Termination or expiration of this Agreement will not relieve either Party of any obligation incurred hereunder prior to such termination or expiration, except that Distributor may be relieved of obligations to make future payments, if applicable. Each Party will be entitled to cancel any outstanding Orders, to the extent Products have not been delivered. Hydraspin shall be entitled to retrieve its Products from the Customers at its sole cost and expense. (b) If termination is the result of a Change of Control, Distributor shall be entitled to receive a onetime payment, within thirty (30) business days of the effective date of the Change of Control, equal to the greater of the following 1) the aggregate amount of the Distributor Share received during the 18 months prior to the effective date of such Change of Control or 2) the aggregate amount of the Distributor Share received on the 30 days prior to the effective date of such Change of Control multiplied by 18.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in 10.3(a) introduces an exception, stating the Distributor 'may be relieved of obligations to make future payments,' directly contradicting the initial assertion that 'Termination or expiration of this Agreement will not relieve either Party of any obligation incurred hereunder prior to such termination or expiration.' Also, the change from 'within three (3) business days' to 'within thirty (30) business days' in 10.3(b) regarding the one-time payment creates a direct conflict in payment terms. This inconsistency aligns with the provided definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction,' where different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, creating in-text contradiction.
